free_text: Nuclear structure of 131Te studied with (n, gamma) and
(d(pol), p) reactions
The 200 mu-g/cm2 thick target was enriched in
130Te (99.3%) and deposited on 4 mu-g/cm2 carbon
backing.
The absolute partial cross sections were calculated
using the integrated deuteron current of the Faraday
cup, which was placed behind the target in the
direction of the beam from the accelerator.
The spectra up to 3.2 MeV were measured at
18 MeV and 12 scattering angles from 11 to 70 degr.
with the Q3D spectrograph and a 1.7 m long focal
plane detector.
Systematic error
Results from (n,gamma) reaction are
included in Entry 31536
Entry approved by Dr. Ivo Tomandl
SD
ADD-RES was added.
Tentative level. Observed only at scattering
angles 17, 20 and 23 degr.
Tentative level. Observed only at scattering
angles 11 and 14 degr.
Interference with the low-spin level at 1855.8keV
Very tentative level.
Tentative level. Observed only at scattering
angles 45, 50 and 70 degr.
While partial cross section distribution prefers
ln = 1 the distribudon of asymmetries is in
agreement with a 3/2+ assignment.
Small contribution from the 128Te(d,p)129Te
Tentative level.
Statistical error
Experimental error.
Data from the table 1 of this paper
The data for the levels above 5203 keV were not
published. The reason  was the lack of  points for the
energy  calibration. The energies of these levels are
from extrapolation of the energy-calibration curve.
Thus, the systematic errors of the energies growth
going  behind  5.2 MeV. It is even difficult to
estimate how it growths. Nevertheless, the authors
very roughly estimate that uncertainties due to the
energy calibration smoothly growths from 0.5 keV at
5.2 MeV up to approx. 3 keV at 5.5 MeV which is the
last point in this SUBENT (comments from the author).
Data were receceived from Dr.I.Tomandl
